page.content: In the Hapag-Lloyd 2Q FY2025 report, the Vessel Age Profile reveals that for the age group 0-5 Years, there are 25 total vessels, comprising 20.0% of the fleet; for 6-10 Years, there are 30 vessels, representing 22.5%; for 11-15 Years, there are 35 vessels, accounting for 25.0%; and for 16+ Years, the total is 0 vessels, making the overall count 90 vessels, which is 67.5%.

vessels, comprising 20.0% of the fleet; for 6-10 Years, there are 30 vessels, representing 22.5%; for 11-15 Years, there are 35

page.content: The scrapping data shows that in 2023, 15 vessels were scrapped at a cost of $12.0M, with a scrapping rate of 10%; in 2024, 18 vessels were scrapped for $14.5M at a rate of 9%; in 2025, 20 vessels will be scrapped costing $16.0M with a rate of 7%, totaling 53 scrapped vessels, costing $42.5M, and a scrapping rate of 26%.

lines.text: The scrapping data shows that in 2023, 15 vessels were scrapped at a cost of $12.0M, with a scrapping rate of 10%; in 2024, 18

page.content: The commodity volumes indicate that grains total 20M tons with a P/E Ratio of 15.5 and a current ratio of 1.8; minerals at 18M tons have a P/E Ratio of 15.0 and a current ratio of 1.6; fertilizers at 12M tons have a P/E Ratio of 14.0 and a current ratio of 1.4; coal at 25M tons has a P/E Ratio of 16.0 and a current ratio of 1.9; cement at 10M tons has a P/E Ratio of 13.0 and a current ratio of 1.5; metals at 22M tons have a P/E Ratio of 17.0 and a current ratio of 1.7, culminating in a total volume of 107M tons and average ratios that include a debt/equity ratio of 0.6, a net margin of 8.0%, and a return on assets of 3.3%.
